name: accounts-push-event
_note: calendar.accounts-push-event is a LENS_ACTIONS (PATH 3) handler that pushes an event back to Google Calendar via writeGoogleCalendarEvent → connectorFetch (server/lib/connector-client.js) — EXTERNAL-IO, so it is marked external_io:true and driven for its network-FREE validation-rejection paths ONLY. ALL of these reject BEFORE connectorFetch is ever reached: unknown accountId → 'account not found'; a pull-only account → reason 'direction_pull_no_push'; a non-google provider → reason 'push_unsupported_provider'; a google push with no event.title → 'event.title required'. Only a valid google two-way push with a real stored OAuth token would hit the network (and returns honest no_token/connector_not_configured when absent). The behavioral suite asserts every pre-network rejection. macro-assassin drives runMacro, which does NOT resolve LENS_ACTIONS, so it never touches this handler; the empty-input case here documents the deterministic 'account not found' rejection. No CC; the SSRF guard lives on the connectorFetch chokepoint.
